paper_metadata.title: Jais and Jais-chat: Arabic-Centric Foundation and Instruction-Tuned Open Generative Large Language Models
paper_metadata.abstract: We introduce Jais and Jais-chat, new state-of-the-art Arabic-centric foundation and instruction-tuned open generative large language models (LLMs). The models are based on the GPT-3 decoder-only architecture and are pretrained on a mixture of Arabic and English texts, including source code in various programming languages. With 13 billion parameters, they demonstrate better knowledge and reasoning capabilities in Arabic than any existing open Arabic and multilingual models by a sizable margin, based on extensive evaluation. Moreover, the models are competitive in English compared to English-centric open models of similar size, despite being trained on much less English data. We provide a detailed description of the training, the tuning, the safety alignment, and the evaluation of the models. We release two open versions of the model -- the foundation Jais model, and an instruction-tuned Jais-chat variant -- with the aim of promoting research on Arabic LLMs. Available at https://huggingface.co/inception-mbzuai/jais-13b-chat

extraction_data.innovation: Arabic-centric large language model trained on a mixed Arabic-English corpus (1:2 ratio) to compensate for limited Arabic data, using a custom BPE tokenizer (Jais tokenizer) optimized for Arabic and English, ALiBi positional encodings for efficient extrapolation to longer contexts, and SwiGLU activation function for improved performance.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Mixed Arabic and English corpus (1:2 ratio of Arabic to English)
extraction_data.hardware_used: Cerebras CS-2 systems within Condor Galaxy 1 (CG-1) AI supercomputer
extraction_data.extension: Custom BPE tokenizer (Jais tokenizer) trained on a balanced Arabic-English corpus to reduce over-segmentation of Arabic words; ALiBi positional encodings for better extrapolation to longer contexts; SwiGLU activation function with adjusted hidden dimensionality to maintain computational efficiency; maximal update parametrization (µP) for stable hyperparameter transfer across model sizes.
extraction_data.research_problem: Developing large language models for Arabic, addressing data scarcity and language-specific challenges such as cliticization and over-segmentation.
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding, dialogue systems, instruction following, general language tasks in Arabic and English
extraction_data.paper_title: Jais and Jais-chat: Arabic-Centric Foundation and Instruction-Tuned Open Generative Large Language Models
